mnemonics: Think of the acronym B.I.A.S.P. for the five risks: Bias, Inaccuracy, Abuse, Security, Privacy
keyPoints: Bias: AI systems can perpetuate and amplify existing societal biases, leading to unfair outcomes.
Inaccuracy: AI can produce incorrect or misleading results, especially when trained on flawed data.
Abuse: Malicious actors can use AI for harmful purposes like disinformation or autonomous weapons.
Security: AI systems are vulnerable to cyberattacks, including data poisoning and model theft.
Privacy: The vast data collection required for AI training and operation poses significant risks to individual privacy.
commonMistakes: Assuming the risks are only technical and ignoring the ethical and societal implications.
Focusing solely on one risk (like bias) while neglecting the interconnected nature of all five.
